appraisal_events.evidence: « Le duc n'était nullement gêné de parler des malaises de sa femme et des siens à un mourant... » puis, à Swann : « Ce sont des ânes. Vous vous portez comme le Pont-Neuf. Vous nous enterrerez tous ! »
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator emphasizes the insensitivity and superficiality of the duke, who prioritizes his small worries and dismisses visitors, then covers his indifference with a hollow bravado addressed to a dying Swann.

status_effects.explanation: Locally, the image of the duke diminishes: he appears tactless and self-centered, his 'bravado' sounding like an unwelcome denial.
